title_en: Predestination, Human Agency, and the Four Levels of Qadar
title_id: Predestinasi, Kehendak Manusia, dan Empat Tingkatan Qadar
content_id:
Bagian substansial dari Shifa al-Alil didedikasikan untuk analisis rinci tentang takdir (qadar) dan hubungannya dengan kehendak bebas manusia. Ini telah menjadi salah satu persoalan sentral dalam teologi Islam sejak periode paling awal: bagaimana Allah bisa menjadi penentu utama dari segala yang terjadi sementara manusia benar-benar bertanggung jawab atas pilihan dan tindakan mereka?

Ibn al-Qayyim memaparkan posisi Sunni Athari tentang qadar melalui kerangka empat tingkatan atau tahapan. Tingkatan pertama adalah ilmu Ilahi: Allah telah mengetahui segala sesuatu yang akan terjadi sejak sebelum penciptaan, dengan pengetahuan yang lengkap dan sempurna yang mencakup semua peristiwa dan semua pelaku. Tingkatan kedua adalah penulisan Ilahi (kitabah): Allah menulis semua yang akan terjadi dalam Lauh Mahfuzh sebelum penciptaan. Tingkatan ketiga adalah kehendak Ilahi (masyiah): segala yang terjadi adalah dengan kehendak Allah, dan tidak ada yang terjadi kecuali dengan kehendak-Nya. Tingkatan keempat adalah penciptaan Ilahi (khalq): Allah menciptakan segala sesuatu, termasuk perbuatan manusia, yang merupakan realitas ciptaan sama seperti semua realitas lainnya.

Gabungan dari empat tingkatan ini tampaknya tidak menyisakan ruang bagi kehendak bebas manusia. Jika Allah mengetahui, telah menulis, menghendaki, dan menciptakan semua perbuatan manusia, dalam pengertian apa agen manusia benar-benar melakukan sesuatu? Respons Ibn al-Qayyim bertumpu pada konsep kasb (perolehan) manusia tetapi mengembangkannya ke arah yang lebih kokoh daripada beberapa perlakuan kalam. Manusia memiliki hasrat, niat, dan pilihan yang benar-benar milik mereka sendiri — bukan paksaan dari luar — meskipun Allah adalah pencipta utama dari semua itu. Kehendak manusia adalah nyata, pilihan manusia adalah nyata, tanggung jawab adalah nyata; penciptaan Ilahi atas semua ini, termasuk kehendak dan pilihan manusia, tidak meniadakan kenyataan dari apa yang diciptakan.

Ia juga membahas pertanyaan mengapa Allah menetapkan dosa dan kejahatan jika Dia tidak menyukainya. Ini adalah pertanyaan paling berat dalam teologi takdir Ilahi. Jawaban Ibn al-Qayyim melibatkan hikmah Ilahi yang menjadi tema sentral buku ini: Allah mengizinkan keberadaan kejahatan dan dosa sebagai bagian dari permadani tujuan yang lebih besar yang, diambil secara keseluruhan, mencerminkan hikmah dan kebaikan. Keberadaan kejahatan memungkinkan terwujudnya kebaikan-kebaikan — kesabaran, taubat, rahmat Ilahi, kontras yang membuat pengakuan atas kebaikan menjadi mungkin — yang tidak dapat ada tanpanya. Ini bukan upaya untuk menjadikan kejahatan itu baik, melainkan argumen bahwa izin atasnya konsisten dengan hikmah Ilahi bila dilihat dalam konteksnya yang utuh.

Bagian dari Shifa al-Alil ini termasuk yang paling kaya dalam literatur teologi Islam tentang persoalan kejahatan dan teologi takdir Ilahi, menggabungkan analisis doktrinal yang cermat dengan bimbingan spiritual praktis.
content_en:
A substantial portion of Shifa al-Alil is devoted to the detailed analysis of divine decree (qadar) and its relationship to human agency. This has been one of the central problems in Islamic theology from the earliest period: how can God be the ultimate determinant of all that occurs while human beings are genuinely responsible for their choices and actions?

Ibn al-Qayyim presents the Sunni Athari position on qadar through the framework of four levels or stages. The first level is divine knowledge (ilm): God has known everything that will occur from before the creation, with complete and perfect knowledge that encompasses all events and all agents. The second level is divine writing (kitabah): God wrote all that would occur in the Preserved Tablet (al-Lawh al-Mahfuz) before the creation. The third level is divine will (mashiyyah): everything that occurs is by God's will, and nothing occurs except by it. The fourth level is divine creation (khalq): God creates all things, including human actions, which are created realities just as all other realities are.

The combination of these four levels appears to leave no room for human agency. If God knows, has written, wills, and creates all human actions, in what sense is the human agent genuinely doing anything? Ibn al-Qayyim's response draws on the concept of human acquisition (kasb) but develops it in a more robust direction than some kalam treatments. Human beings have genuine desires, intentions, and choices that are truly their own — not external impositions — even though God is the ultimate creator of all. The human will is real, the human choice is real, the responsibility is real; the divine creation of all of this, including the human will and choice, does not negate the reality of what is created.

He also addresses the question of why God decrees sin and evil if He disapproves of them. This is the hardest question in the theology of divine decree. Ibn al-Qayyim's answer involves the divine wisdom that is the book's central theme: God permits the existence of evil and sin as part of a larger tapestry of purposes that, taken as a whole, reflects wisdom and goodness. The existence of evil allows for the realization of goods — patience, repentance, divine mercy, the contrast that makes the recognition of good possible — that could not exist without it. This is not an attempt to make evil good but an argument that its permission is consistent with divine wisdom when viewed in its full context.

This section of Shifa al-Alil is among the richest in Islamic theological literature on the problem of evil and the theology of divine decree, combining careful doctrinal analysis with practical spiritual guidance.
content_ar: A substantial portion of Shifa al-Alil is devoted to the detailed analysis of divine decree (القدر) and its relationship to human agency. This has been one of the المحوري problems in Islamic theology from the earliest period: how can God be the ultimate determinant of all that occurs بينما human beings are genuinely responsible for their choices and actions? ابن القيم presents the Sunni الأثري position on القدر through the framework of four levels or stages. The أولاً level is divine العلم (ilm): God has known everything that will occur from قبل the creation, with complete and perfect العلم that encompasses all events and all agents. The ثانياً level is divine writing (kitabah): God wrote all that would occur in the Preserved Tablet (al-Lawh al-Mahfuz) قبل the creation. The ثالثاً level is divine will (mashiyyah): everything that occurs is by God's will, and nothing occurs except by it. The رابعاً level is divine creation (khalq): God creates all things, بما في ذلك human actions, which are created realities just as all other realities are. The combination of these four levels appears to leave no room for human agency. إذا God knows, has written, wills, and creates all human actions, in what sense is the human agent genuinely doing anything? ابن القيم's response draws on the concept of human acquisition (kasb) but develops it in a more robust direction than some علم الكلام treatments. Human beings have genuine desires, intentions, and choices that are truly their own — not external impositions — حتى وإن God is the ultimate creator of all. The human will is real, the human choice is real, the المسؤولية is real; the divine creation of all of this, بما في ذلك the human will and choice, does not negate the reality of what is created. He أيضاً addresses the question of why God decrees الذنب and الشر إذا He disapproves of them. This is the hardest question in the theology of divine decree. ابن القيم's answer involves the divine الحكمة that is the الكتاب's المحوري theme: God permits the existence of الشر and الذنب as part of a larger tapestry of purposes that, taken as a whole, reflects الحكمة and goodness. The existence of الشر allows for the realization of goods — الصبر, التوبة, divine الرحمة, the contrast that makes the recognition of الخير possible — that could not exist without it. This is not an attempt to make الشر الخير but an argument that its permission is consistent with divine الحكمة عندما viewed in its full context. This الفصل of Shifa al-Alil is among the richest in Islamic theological literature on the problem of الشر and the theology of divine decree, combining careful doctrinal analysis with practical spiritual الهداية.
